title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Poirot explains to Mrs. Oliver that Roya Drake is the killer. He reveals that Drake, acting like a "Lady Macbeth," orchestrated a scene to explain why she was wet after drowning Joyce Reynolds. Drake dropped a vase of flowers to splash herself with water, creating a witness in Miss Whitck to validate her appearance. Poirot notes that Drake and Michael Garfield were partners in crime, motivated by Garfield's obsession with creating a beautiful garden on a Greek island using the Llewellyn-Smith estate.

The motive involved a complex scheme regarding a will. Garfield and Drake intended to inherit the estate by using a forged codicil that was designed to be easily debunked, thereby ensuring the original will—which favored Drake—remained in effect. To protect this plan, they eliminated Olga Seminova, who knew about the genuine codicil, and Leslie Farrar, who was involved in the forgery. Poirot discovered Olga's body in a wishing well in the Quarry Woods after hearing a nursery rhyme reference. He also deduces that the death of little Leopold was a move to silence him because he knew too much.

Poirot describes Garfield as a "Lucifer" figure—a man obsessed with beauty who was willing to sacrifice anything. Judith Butler reveals she is the mother of Garfield's daughter, Miranda, having fled him years ago due to his cold nature. Poirot explains that Garfield planned to sacrifice Miranda in a ritualistic ceremony at the Canterbury Stone Circle to achieve his "new Eden," a plan symbolized by a drawing of her titled "Iphigenia." Ultimately, Garfield dies by his own hand at the stone circle, and Miranda survives.
